title: eugenia cotinifolia subsp. codyensis
intro: Eugenia cotinifolia subsp. codyensis, commonly known as Cody Eugenia, is a plant species of the Myrtaceae family. It is a type of shrub that is native to the mountainous regions of New Mexico, USA, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. Eugenia cotinifolia subsp. codyensis is known for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce pain, and protect against oxidative stress. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, and can be consumed as a dietary supplement to support overall health and wellness.
